summary: Iteration 271 - Enlightenment Cafe Tiered Render Delegate Testing. Parchment mode (default) works correctly. CRITICAL: Nebula mode (3D WebGL) throws runtime errors when activated - 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' and 'TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object'. These errors appear related to the Line component from @react-three/drei.

integration_issues.issue: Switching to Nebula mode causes runtime errors: 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' and 'TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object'. The errors appear in the webpack-dev-server-client-overlay iframe, blocking further interaction.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix Nebula mode runtime errors - The Line component from @react-three/drei is causing 'Cannot set x-line-number' error. This is a known JSX namespace conflict issue. Consider: (1) Using a different approach for gold thread connections (e.g., custom BufferGeometry with LineBasicMaterial), (2) Wrapping Line component in error boundary, or (3) Conditionally rendering gold threads only when features.webgl is true AND device supports it
The error 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' suggests a component lifecycle issue during mount/unmount - check if all refs and objects are properly initialized before use
critical_code_review_comments: In Islands.js GoldThreadMesh component (lines 254-299): The Line component from @react-three/drei may have JSX namespace conflicts. The points array format [[0,0,0], [x,y,z]] should work, but there may be an issue with how the component handles the props during initial render

Scene.js: The NebulaScene component has proper conditional rendering based on webglEnabled, but the error occurs after the component mounts - suggesting the issue is in the child components (Islands/GoldThreadMesh)

success_rate.frontend: 70% - Parchment mode works, Nebula mode has critical errors

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 271 tested the Enlightenment Cafe Tiered Render Delegate. Parchment mode (Essential/2D) is the default and works correctly - no 3D rendering, all UI elements accessible. Nebula mode (Premium/3D WebGL) has CRITICAL runtime errors when activated. The errors are: (1) 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' - likely a JSX namespace conflict with the Line component from @react-three/drei, (2) 'TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object' - component lifecycle issue. The errors appear in the webpack-dev-server-client-overlay and block further interaction. Test data-testids: cafe-settings-toggle, cafe-settings-panel, tier-parchment, tier-nebula, nebula-toggle-compact, nebula-quick-toggle, emergency-shutoff, nebula-scene.

parchment_default.details: Parchment is the default view tier, localStorage shows viewTier='parchment'

nebula_toggle_visible.details: Nebula toggle button visible in bottom-left corner with data-testid='nebula-quick-toggle' and 'nebula-toggle-compact'

cafe_settings_panel.details: Cafe Settings panel opens correctly with Visualization section showing Parchment vs Nebula options

nebula_option_click.details: Clicking Nebula option changes viewTier to 'nebula' in localStorage, but causes runtime errors that block the UI

emergency_shutoff_visible.details: Emergency Shut-Off button visible and accessible in top-left corner (top=8, left=8)

parchment_no_errors.details: App loads without runtime errors in Parchment mode

dashboard_with_auth.details: Dashboard page loads correctly after authentication with user grad_test_522@test.com

nebula_scene_not_rendered_in_parchment.details: Nebula Scene element not found in DOM when in Parchment mode (correct behavior)

nebula_runtime_errors.error: R3F: Cannot set 'x-line-number'. Ensure it is an object before setting 'line-number'.
nebula_runtime_errors.likely_cause: JSX namespace conflict between Three.js line element and HTML/SVG line element in @react-three/drei Line component

nebula_runtime_errors.error: TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object
nebula_runtime_errors.likely_cause: Component lifecycle issue - trying to access properties on undefined/null object during unmount or re-render
